analysis.classification_reasoning: Week closed +35% with 70% retention of the $18.19 peak, and the Thursday breakout had a documented catalyst — a proposed $100M CHIPS Act funding deal plus Trump-administration equity-stake news lifting the entire quantum cohort. Volume confirmed it: Thursday 1.94M shares and Friday 1.57M vs a prior ~250–400K baseline (5–7x relative volume) with MACD bullish across the late-week scans. Friday gave back some intraday gains (faded from +31% prints to +11% close) but held well above the pre-news $11 base, which is consolidation, not failure.
analysis.prediction_rationale: Base case is a continuation higher with chop: government-backed quantum narrative + named $100M deal is a re-rating catalyst, and the stock is holding above the breakout pivot at ~$13.40 with multi-million-share confirmation. The intraday fade from $18.19 to $16.35 signals near-term profit-taking and likely a retest of the $14–15 gap zone before another leg. Risk is an ATM/capital raise filing — typical post-spike dilution playbook for sub-$20 quantum names — which would cap the move quickly.

analysis.classification_reasoning: This wasn't bullish momentum — it was a violent two-way week. MU crashed from 818.67 (5/11 high) to 652.23 (5/19 low), a -20% drawdown, then bounced ~15% off the low to close 751.09. Week return of +0.53% with retention of peak just 6% confirms the upper range is rejected; the "rally" into Friday is a mean-reversion bounce on declining volume (Friday 467K vs. 10-day avg ~1M), not accumulation. analysis.prediction_rationale: Base case is a 680–780 chop over the next 2–3 weeks. The 818 high is well-defended supply, the 652 low established a tradable floor, and Friday's anemic 467K volume on a green day signals the bounce is running out of fuel before any real catalyst. Direction resolves on FQ3 earnings in late June; until then, expect noise around the 720–760 midpoint with bearish MACD capping breakouts.
